---
title: "Resource Optimizer"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
  pdf:
    keep-tex: false
  epub: default
---

<!-- Resource Optimizer Template
     Find the optimal resource size/tier based on usage patterns.

     Usage: Copy this template into your chapter .qmd file, then customize
     the tier definitions, scoring weights, and usage inputs.

     HTML: Interactive inputs with reactive recommendation engine.
     PDF/EPUB: Static fallback table showing tier recommendations.
-->

::: {.callout-note}
## Resource Optimizer
Find the best resource tier for your workload. Adjust the sliders to match
your usage pattern and see which option provides the best fit.
:::

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// --- Workload Inputs ---

viewof peakCPU = Inputs.range([10, 100], {
  value: 65,
  step: 5,
  label: "Peak CPU utilization (%)"
})

viewof avgMemoryGB = Inputs.range([1, 128], {
  value: 16,
  step: 1,
  label: "Average memory usage (GB)"
})

viewof dailyRequests = Inputs.range([1000, 10000000], {
  value: 500000,
  step: 10000,
  label: "Daily requests"
})

viewof storageNeedGB = Inputs.range([10, 2000], {
  value: 200,
  step: 10,
  label: "Storage needed (GB)"
})

viewof priority = Inputs.select(
  ["cost", "performance", "balanced"],
  { value: "balanced", label: "Optimization priority" }
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// --- Tier Definitions ---
// Customize these for your infrastructure scenario.

tiers = [
  {
    name: "Small",
    cpu: 2, memory: 4, maxRequests: 100000, storage: 50,
    monthlyPrice: 75,
    description: "Dev/test workloads"
  },
  {
    name: "Medium",
    cpu: 4, memory: 16, maxRequests: 1000000, storage: 200,
    monthlyPrice: 250,
    description: "Standard production"
  },
  {
    name: "Large",
    cpu: 8, memory: 32, maxRequests: 5000000, storage: 500,
    monthlyPrice: 600,
    description: "High-traffic production"
  },
  {
    name: "X-Large",
    cpu: 16, memory: 64, maxRequests: 10000000, storage: 1000,
    monthlyPrice: 1100,
    description: "Enterprise / heavy compute"
  },
  {
    name: "2X-Large",
    cpu: 32, memory: 128, maxRequests: 25000000, storage: 2000,
    monthlyPrice: 2000,
    description: "Maximum throughput"
  }
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// --- Scoring Engine ---

weights = ({
  cost: { fit: 0.2, headroom: 0.1, cost: 0.7 },
  performance: { fit: 0.5, headroom: 0.4, cost: 0.1 },
  balanced: { fit: 0.4, headroom: 0.3, cost: 0.3 }
})[priority]

scoredTiers = {
  const requiredCPU = peakCPU / 100 * 4;  // normalize to vCPUs needed
  const maxPrice = Math.max(...tiers.map(t => t.monthlyPrice));

  return tiers.map(tier => {
    // Fit score: how well does the tier match requirements (1 = perfect)
    const cpuFit = Math.min(1, tier.cpu / Math.max(requiredCPU, 1));
    const memFit = Math.min(1, tier.memory / Math.max(avgMemoryGB, 1));
    const reqFit = Math.min(1, tier.maxRequests / Math.max(dailyRequests, 1));
    const stoFit = Math.min(1, tier.storage / Math.max(storageNeedGB, 1));
    const fitScore = (cpuFit + memFit + reqFit + stoFit) / 4;

    // Headroom: penalize under-provisioned, prefer moderate headroom
    const cpuHeadroom = tier.cpu / Math.max(requiredCPU, 1);
    const memHeadroom = tier.memory / Math.max(avgMemoryGB, 1);
    const headroomScore = Math.min(1, (cpuHeadroom + memHeadroom) / 4);

    // Cost efficiency: lower is better
    const costScore = 1 - (tier.monthlyPrice / maxPrice);

    // Check if tier meets minimum requirements
    const meetsMinimum = tier.memory >= avgMemoryGB && tier.maxRequests >= dailyRequests && tier.storage >= storageNeedGB;

    const weighted = fitScore * weights.fit + headroomScore * weights.headroom + costScore * weights.cost;
    const finalScore = meetsMinimum ? weighted : weighted * 0.3;

    return {
      ...tier,
      fitScore: Math.round(fitScore * 100),
      headroomScore: Math.round(headroomScore * 100),
      costScore: Math.round(costScore * 100),
      totalScore: Math.round(finalScore * 100),
      meetsMinimum,
      recommended: false
    };
  }).sort((a, b) => b.totalScore - a.totalScore).map((t, i) => ({
    ...t,
    recommended: i === 0
  }));
}

recommended = scoredTiers[0]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// --- Recommendation Output ---

fmt = d3.format("$,.0f")

html`<div class="ojs-calculator">
  <div class="ojs-recommendation">
    <div class="ojs-recommendation-badge">Recommended</div>
    <h4>${recommended.name}</h4>
    <p>${recommended.description}</p>
    <p class="ojs-recommendation-price">${fmt(recommended.monthlyPrice)}/month</p>
    <p class="ojs-recommendation-detail">
      ${recommended.cpu} vCPU | ${recommended.memory} GB RAM |
      ${d3.format(",")(recommended.maxRequests)} req/day | ${recommended.storage} GB storage
    </p>
  </div>

  <table class="ojs-results-table">
    <thead>
      <tr>
        <th>Tier</th>
        <th>Specs</th>
        <th>Price</th>
        <th>Fit</th>
        <th>Headroom</th>
        <th>Cost Eff.</th>
        <th>Score</th>
      </tr>
    </thead>
    <tbody>
      ${scoredTiers.map(t => html`<tr class="${t.recommended ? 'ojs-highlight-row' : ''} ${!t.meetsMinimum ? 'ojs-insufficient-row' : ''}">
        <td><strong>${t.name}</strong>${t.recommended ? html` <span class="ojs-badge">Best</span>` : ''}</td>
        <td>${t.cpu} vCPU / ${t.memory} GB</td>
        <td>${fmt(t.monthlyPrice)}</td>
        <td>${t.fitScore}%</td>
        <td>${t.headroomScore}%</td>
        <td>${t.costScore}%</td>
        <td><strong>${t.totalScore}%</strong></td>
      </tr>`)}
    </tbody>
  </table>
  <p class="ojs-note">${scoredTiers.filter(t => !t.meetsMinimum).length > 0 ?
    "Grayed-out tiers do not meet minimum resource requirements." : ""}</p>
</div>`
```

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}
**Resource Recommendation (Default: Balanced Priority)**

Workload: 65% peak CPU, 16 GB memory, 500K daily requests, 200 GB storage.

| Tier | vCPU | Memory | Requests/day | Storage | Price | Score |
|------|------|--------|-------------|---------|-------|-------|
| **Medium** | 4 | 16 GB | 1M | 200 GB | $250/mo | **Best** |
| Large | 8 | 32 GB | 5M | 500 GB | $600/mo | Good |
| X-Large | 16 | 64 GB | 10M | 1 TB | $1,100/mo | Over-provisioned |
| Small | 2 | 4 GB | 100K | 50 GB | $75/mo | Under-provisioned |

**Recommendation: Medium tier** at $250/month provides the best balance of
fit, headroom, and cost for this workload. Adjust values in the interactive
HTML version.
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="epub"}
**Resource Recommendation (Default: Balanced Priority)**

Workload: 65% peak CPU, 16 GB memory, 500K daily requests, 200 GB storage.

| Tier | vCPU | Memory | Requests/day | Storage | Price | Score |
|------|------|--------|-------------|---------|-------|-------|
| **Medium** | 4 | 16 GB | 1M | 200 GB | $250/mo | **Best** |
| Large | 8 | 32 GB | 5M | 500 GB | $600/mo | Good |
| X-Large | 16 | 64 GB | 10M | 1 TB | $1,100/mo | Over-provisioned |
| Small | 2 | 4 GB | 100K | 50 GB | $75/mo | Under-provisioned |

**Recommendation: Medium tier** at $250/month provides the best balance of
fit, headroom, and cost for this workload. Adjust values in the interactive
HTML version.
:::
